ogTitle: Pelican Point vs Peppermint Grove: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Pelican Point and Peppermint Grove.
heading: Pelican Point vs Peppermint Grove — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pelican Point, Western Australia and Peppermint Grove, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Peppermint Grove has a lower overall climate risk score (42/100) compared to Pelican Point (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pelican Point or Peppermint Grove safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pelican Point has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Peppermint Grove scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pelican Point scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Peppermint Grove scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pelican Point or Peppermint Grove?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Peppermint Grove has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
